headline: A Volcano Turns a Town Into a Cemetery

beats.caption: When the Fuego volcano erupted in June, its pyroclastic flows buried much of the Guatemalan village of San Miguel Los Lotes.
The Volcán de Fuego exploded on June 3, throwing a billowing tower of ash more than a mile into the sky. Burning lava, rocks and gas cascaded down its slopes.
Nobody was prepared for the explosion at lunchtime, when a pyroclastic flow as hot as 1,600°F engulfed the village in deep ash.
San Miguel is a ghost town, life stopped in an instant. This truck and a nearby shed were buried in about three and a half feet of ash — scanned where they came to rest.
